file.content:
# Plan: Migrate from Travis CI to GitHub Actions with Self-Hosted Runners

## Context
Travis CI is slow (~12 min builds) and uses cross-compilation. Moving to GitHub Actions with self-hosted runners that build natively on the target architecture, then push to Harbor. Both ARM64 and x86 images are built in parallel on push to main, so either architecture is instantly available when promoting to any environment.

## Architecture

```
Push to main
    ├── Runner on UAT (ARM64) → builds ARM64 image → pushes to Harbor
    └── Runner on Prod (x86)  → builds x86 image  → pushes to Harbor
                                       │
                              Both available in Harbor
                                       │
              ┌────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
              ▼                        ▼                      ▼
     Deploy Staging (ARM64)    Deploy UAT (ARM64)    Deploy Prod (x86)
     pulls ARM64 image         pulls ARM64 image     pulls x86 image
```

## Servers & Runners

| Server | IP | Arch | Runner Label | Builds For |
|--------|-----|------|-------------|------------|
| Prod | 31.97.102.48 | x86_64 | `self-hosted, linux, x64` | x86 images |
| UAT | 92.4.83.28 | x86_64 | `self-hosted, linux, x64` | x86 images (already has runner) |
| Staging | 129.213.82.37 | ARM64 | `self-hosted, linux, arm64` | ARM64 images |

**Wait** — Staging is ARM64, UAT and Prod are x86. So:
- ARM64 builds happen on **Staging server** (the only ARM64 box)
- x86 builds happen on **Prod or UAT server**

## Steps

### 1. Install GitHub Actions runners on servers that don't have them

**Staging (129.213.82.37)** — ARM64, needs runner installed
**Prod (31.97.102.48)** — x86, needs runner installed
**UAT (92.4.83.28)** — already has runner installed

Register runners with labels:
- Staging: `self-hosted, linux, arm64, staging`
- UAT: `self-hosted, linux, x64, uat`
- Prod: `self-hosted, linux, x64, prod`

### 2. GitHub Actions Workflow for Blog

**File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`**

```yaml
name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, production, uat]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, x64]  # UAT or Prod
    steps:
      - checkout
      - setup Go 1.26
      - go test ./... -coverprofile=coverage.out
      - upload coverage artifact

  build-arm64:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, arm64]  # Staging server
    steps:
      - checkout
      - docker build -t harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-${{ github.sha }} .
      - docker push harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-${{ github.sha }}
      - docker tag ... harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-latest
      - docker push harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-latest

  build-x64:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, x64, prod]  # Prod server
    steps:
      - checkout
      - docker build -t harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:x64-${{ github.sha }} .
      - docker push harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:x64-${{ github.sha }}
      - docker tag ... harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:x64-latest
      - docker push harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:x64-latest

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-arm64
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, arm64]  # Staging server (same box)
    steps:
      - docker pull harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-${{ github.sha }}
      - docker compose up -d (using pulled image)
      - health check

  deploy-uat:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/uat'
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, x64, uat]
    steps:
      - pull x64 image from Harbor
      - docker compose up -d
      - health check

  deploy-production:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/production'
    runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, x64, prod]
    steps:
      - pull x64 image from Harbor
      - docker compose up -d
      - health check
```

### 3. Same pattern for TaskAI

Separate workflow in taskai repo, same runner labels, same Harbor push/pull pattern.

### 4. Speed Optimizations

- **Docker layer caching**: Use `--cache-from` with previous image
- **Parallel builds**: ARM64 and x86 build simultaneously after tests pass
- **Tests on fastest server**: Run on x86 (UAT/Prod — faster CPU)
- **No cross-compilation**: Native builds are 3-5x faster
- **Image already on deploy server**: Build + deploy on same box for staging/prod
- **Slim test stage**: Only run tests, don't build images, on PRs

### 5. Secrets Migration

Move all Travis secrets to GitHub repo secrets:
- `HARBOR_AUTH` (or split into `HARBOR_USER` + `HARBOR_PASS`)
- SSH keys (not needed — runners ARE on the servers)
- Per-env DB passwords, JWT secrets, OAuth credentials
- DD_API_KEY, SONAR_TOKEN

### 6. Decommission Travis

After GitHub Actions is working:
- Remove `.travis.yml` from both repos
- Cancel Travis CI subscription
- Keep `deployment/scripts/` (reuse for GHA steps)

## Projects to Migrate
1. Blog (anshumanbiswas.com)
2. TaskAI (taskai.cc)
3. Any other projects on Travis

## Verification
1. Push to main → both images build in parallel, staging auto-deploys
2. Push to uat branch → UAT deploys from Harbor x64 image
3. Push to production branch → Prod deploys from Harbor x64 image
4. PR → only tests run (no build/deploy)
5. Build times < 5 minutes total (test + build + deploy)
6. `curl https://anshumanbiswas.com/api/version` shows correct commit

content.content:
=== /Users/anshumanbiswas/play/blog/.travis.yml ===
# Travis CI configuration for Blog (anshumanbiswas.com)
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digest through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   BLOG_PG_PASSWORD, BLOG_SESSION_SECRET, BLOG_API_TOKEN
#   STAGING_ENCRYPTION_KEY, UAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY, PRODUCTION_ENCRYPTION_KEY
#   GH_CLIENT_ID, GH_CLIENT_SECRET, OAUTH_STATE_SECRET
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── Go Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Blog Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
        - PG_HOST=127.0.0.1

=== /Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/.travis.yml ===
# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline
#
# FAST DEPLOY: Tests + deploy run in parallel. Deploy builds images directly
# on the target server (native arch, no cross-compilation, no registry).
# Tests run alongside deploy — if tests fail, the build is marked failed
# but staging already has the code (fix-forward).
#
# Pipeline:
#   main branch:       test + deploy-staging (parallel) → ~5 min
#   production branch: deploy-production → ~4 min (build on server)
#   uat branch:        deploy-uat → ~4 min (build on server)
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password") — kept for rollback
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: deploy-and-test
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: test-only
    if: type = pull_request
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # MAIN BRANCH: tests + staging deploy run IN PARALLEL (same stage)
    # Deploy builds natively on the staging server — no cross-compilation.
    # Tests run alongside — if they fail, build is marked failed but
    # staging is already deployed (fix-forward philosophy).

=== /Users/anshumanbiswas/play/flagtgl/.travis.yml ===
# Travis CI configuration for FlagTGL
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digest through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/flagtgl).
#
# Required env vars (set in Travis CI Settings):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   FLAGS_DB_PASSWORD, FLAGS_JWT_SECRET
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── Backend + SDK Tests (single Go job) ──────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Backend + SDK Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod

=== /Users/anshumanbiswas/play/folioworth.com/.travis.yml ===
# Travis CI configuration for FolioWorth
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/folioworth-{backend,frontend}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   FOLIOWORTH_PG_PASSWORD, FOLIOWORTH_JWT_SECRET, FOLIOWORTH_TINGO_API_KEY
#   CF_ORIGIN_CERT_BASE64, CF_ORIGIN_KEY_BASE64
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:

# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline
#
# FAST DEPLOY: Tests + deploy run in parallel. Deploy builds images directly
# on the target server (native arch, no cross-compilation, no registry).
# Tests run alongside deploy — if tests fail, the build is marked failed
# but staging already has the code (fix-forward).
#
# Pipeline:
#   main branch:       test + deploy-staging (parallel) → ~5 min
#   production branch: deploy-production → ~4 min (build on server)
#   uat branch:        deploy-uat → ~4 min (build on server)
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64

#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password") — kept for rollback
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: deploy-and-test
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: test-only

  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:

    # Deploy builds natively on the staging server — no cross-compilation.
    # Tests run alongside — if they fail, build is marked failed but
    # staging is already deployed (fix-forward philosophy).

1→# CI/CD Pipeline for Blog (anshumanbiswas.com)
     2→#
     3→# Native multi-arch builds on self-hosted runners — no cross-compilation.
     4→# Push to main builds ARM64 + x64 images in parallel, pushes both to Harbor,
     5→# then auto-deploys staging (ARM64). Promoting to prod uses the already-built
     6→# x64 image from Harbor (no rebuild needed).
     7→#
     8→# Runners:
     9→#   ARM64 runner: Staging server (129.213.82.37) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, ARM64
    10→#   x64 runner:   Prod server (31.97.102.48) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, X64, prod
    11→#   x64 runner:   UAT server (92.4.83.28) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, X64, uat
    12→#
    13→# Flow:
    14→#   main push       → test → build ARM64 + x64 (parallel) → deploy staging (ARM64)
    15→#   uat push        → deploy UAT using arm64-latest from Harbor
    16→#   production push → deploy prod using x64-latest from Harbor
    17→#
    18→# Required secrets:
    19→#   HARBOR_AUTH                   — base64("username:password") for Harbor registry
    20→#   STAGING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY       — raw PEM SSH key for staging server
    21→#   UAT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY           — raw PEM SSH key for UAT server
    22→#   SSH_PRIVATE_KEY               — raw PEM SSH key for production server
    23→#   BLOG_PG_PASSWORD, BLOG_SESSION_SECRET, BLOG_API_TOKEN
    24→#   ENCRYPTION_KEY (or per-env: STAGING_ENCRYPTION_KEY, UAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY, PRODUCTION_ENCRYPTION_KEY)
    25→#   GH_CLIENT_ID, GH_CLIENT_SECRET, OAUTH_STATE_SECRET
    26→#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE
    27→
    28→name: CI/CD Pipeline
    29→
    30→on:
    31→  push:
    32→    branches: [main, uat, production]
    33→  pull_request:
    34→    branches: [main]
    35→
    36→concurrency:
    37→  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
    38→  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
    39→
    40→jobs:
    41→  # ── Go Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42→  test:
    43→    name: "Blog Tests (Go)"
    44→    runs-on: ${{ vars.CI_RUNNER || 'self-hosted' }}
    45→    if: |
    46→      (vars.CI_ENABLED || 'true') == 'true' &&
    47→      (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.event_name == 'pull_request')
    48→
    49→    services:
    50→      postgres:
    51→        image: postgres:15-alpine
    52→        env:
    53→          POSTGRES_USER: blog
    54→          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
    55→          POSTGRES_DB: blog
    56→        ports:
    57→          - 5434:5432
    58→        options: >-
    59→          --health-cmd pg_isready
    60→          --health-interval 10s
    61→          --health-timeout 5s
    62→          --health-retries 5
    63→
    64→    env:
    65→      PG_HOST: 127.0.0.1
    66→      PG_PORT: 5434
    67→      PG_USER: blog
    68→      PG_PASSWORD: testpass
    69→      PG_DB: blog
    70→      GOTOOLCHAIN: local
    71→
    72→    steps:
    73→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    74→
    75→      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
    76→        with:
    77→          go-version: '1.26'
    78→          cache: true
    79→
    80→      - name: Run database migrations
    81→        run: |
    82→          ARCH=$(uname -m)
    83→          [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] && MIGRATE_ARCH="arm64" || MIGRATE_ARCH="amd64"
    84→          curl -sL "https://github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/releases/download/v4.17.0/migrate.linux-${MIGRATE_ARCH}.tar.gz" | tar xz
    85→          chmod +x migrate
    86→          ./migrate -source file://migrations \
    87→            -database "postgresql://blog:testpass@127.0.0.1:5434/blog?sslmode=disable" up
    88→
    89→      - run: go vet ./...
    90→
    91→      - name: Run tests
    92→        run: go test -v -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...
    93→        timeout-minutes: 10
    94→
    95→      - run: go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
    96→
    97→      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    98→        with:
    99→          name: coverage
   100→          path: coverage.out
   101→          retention-days: 30
   102→
   103→  # ── SonarQube (allowed to fail) ────────────────────────────────────────
   104→  sonarqube:
   105→    name: "SonarQube Analysis"
   106→    runs-on: ${{ vars.CI_RUNNER || 'self-hosted' }}
   107→    needs: test
   108→    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
   109→    continue-on-error: true
   110→
   111→    steps:
   112→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   113→        with:
   114→          fetch-depth: 0
   115→
   116→      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
   117→        with:
   118→          name: coverage
   119→        continue-on-error: true
   120→
   121→      - name: Run SonarQube
   122→        env:
   123→          SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
   124→        run: |
   125→          ARCH=$(uname -m)
   126→          SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
   127→          if [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ]; then
   128→            SONAR_ZIP="sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux-aarch64.zip"
   129→          else
   130→            SONAR_ZIP="sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
   131→          fi
   132→          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip \
   133→            "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/$SONAR_ZIP"
   134→          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
   135→          SCANNER_DIR=$(ls -d sonar-scanner-*)
   136→          "$SCANNER_DIR/bin/sonar-scanner" \
   137→            -Dsonar.host.url="https://sonar.taskai.cc" \
   138→            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
   139→            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"
   140→
   141→  # ── Build ARM64 Image (native on ARM64 runner) ─────────────────────────
   142→  build-arm64:
   143→    name: "Build ARM64 Image"
   144→    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, ARM64]
   145→    needs: test
   146→    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
   147→    outputs:
   148→      tag: ${{ steps.build.outputs.tag }}
   149→
   150→    steps:
   151→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   152→
   153→      - name: Build and push to Harbor
   154→        id: build
   155→        env:
   156→          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
   157→        run: |
   158→          TAG="arm64-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
   159→          IMAGE="harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog"
   160→
   161→          # Docker login to Harbor
   162→          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1 > /tmp/.hu
   163→          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2 > /tmp/.hp
   164→          docker login harbor.biswas.me -u "$(cat /tmp/.hu)" -p "$(cat /tmp/.hp)"
   165→          rm -f /tmp/.hu /tmp/.hp
   166→
   167→          # Native build (no cross-compilation)
   168→          docker build --cache-from "$IMAGE:arm64-latest" -t "$IMAGE:$TAG" .
   169→          docker push "$IMAGE:$TAG"
   170→
   171→          # Tag as arm64-latest
   172→          docker tag "$IMAGE:$TAG" "$IMAGE:arm64-latest"
   173→          docker push "$IMAGE:arm64-latest"
   174→
   175→          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   176→          echo "Built and pushed $IMAGE:$TAG (ARM64)"
   177→
   178→  # ── Build x64 Image (native on x64 runner) ───────────────────────────
   179→  build-x64:
   180→    name: "Build x64 Image"
   181→    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64, prod]
   182→    needs: test
   183→    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'

   190→      - name: Build and push to Harbor

   198→          # Docker login to Harbor
   199→          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1 > /tmp/.hu
   200→          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2 > /tmp/.hp
   201→          docker login harbor.biswas.me -u "$(cat /tmp/.hu)" -p "$(cat /tmp/.hp)"

   204→          # Native build (no cross-compilation)

   215→  # ── Deploy Staging (ARM64) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   216→  deploy-staging:

file.content:
# CI/CD Pipeline for Blog (anshumanbiswas.com)
#
# Native multi-arch builds on self-hosted runners — no cross-compilation.
# Push to main builds ARM64 + x64 images in parallel, pushes both to Harbor,
# then auto-deploys staging (ARM64). Promoting to prod uses the already-built
# x64 image from Harbor (no rebuild needed).
#
# Runners:
#   ARM64 runner: Staging server (129.213.82.37) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, ARM64
#   x64 runner:   Prod server (31.97.102.48) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, X64, prod
#   x64 runner:   UAT server (92.4.83.28) — labels: self-hosted, Linux, X64, uat
#
# Flow:
#   main push       → test → build ARM64 + x64 (parallel) → deploy staging (ARM64)
#   uat push        → deploy UAT using arm64-latest from Harbor
#   production push → deploy prod using x64-latest from Harbor
#
# Required secrets:
#   HARBOR_AUTH                   — base64("username:password") for Harbor registry
#   STAGING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY       — raw PEM SSH key for staging server
#   UAT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY           — raw PEM SSH key for UAT server
#   SSH_PRIVATE_KEY               — raw PEM SSH key for production server
#   BLOG_PG_PASSWORD, BLOG_SESSION_SECRET, BLOG_API_TOKEN
#   ENCRYPTION_KEY (or per-env: STAGING_ENCRYPTION_KEY, UAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY, PRODUCTION_ENCRYPTION_KEY)
#   GH_CLIENT_ID, GH_CLIENT_SECRET, OAUTH_STATE_SECRET
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, uat, production]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  # ── Go Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: "Blog Tests (Go)"
    runs-on: ${{ vars.CI_RUNNER || 'self-hosted' }}
    if: |
      (vars.CI_ENABLED || 'true') == 'true' &&
      (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.event_name == 'pull_request')

    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: blog
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
          POSTGRES_DB: blog
        ports:
          - 5434:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

    env:
      PG_HOST: 127.0.0.1
      PG_PORT: 5434
      PG_USER: blog
      PG_PASSWORD: testpass
      PG_DB: blog
      GOTOOLCHAIN: local

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.26'
          cache: true

      - name: Run database migrations
        run: |
          ARCH=$(uname -m)
          [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] && MIGRATE_ARCH="arm64" || MIGRATE_ARCH="amd64"
          curl -sL "https://github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/releases/download/v4.17.0/migrate.linux-${MIGRATE_ARCH}.tar.gz" | tar xz
          chmod +x migrate
          ./migrate -source file://migrations \
            -database "postgresql://blog:testpass@127.0.0.1:5434/blog?sslmode=disable" up

      - run: go vet ./...

      - name: Run tests
        run: go test -v -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...
        timeout-minutes: 10

      - run: go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage
          path: coverage.out
          retention-days: 30

  # ── SonarQube (allowed to fail) ────────────────────────────────────────
  sonarqube:
    name: "SonarQube Analysis"
    runs-on: ${{ vars.CI_RUNNER || 'self-hosted' }}
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    continue-on-error: true

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Run SonarQube
        env:
          SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          ARCH=$(uname -m)
          SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          if [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ]; then
            SONAR_ZIP="sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux-aarch64.zip"
          else
            SONAR_ZIP="sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          fi
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip \
            "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/$SONAR_ZIP"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          SCANNER_DIR=$(ls -d sonar-scanner-*)
          "$SCANNER_DIR/bin/sonar-scanner" \
            -Dsonar.host.url="https://sonar.taskai.cc" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

  # ── Build ARM64 Image (native on ARM64 runner) ─────────────────────────
  build-arm64:
    name: "Build ARM64 Image"
    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, ARM64]
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.build.outputs.tag }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build and push to Harbor
        id: build
        env:
          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
        run: |
          TAG="arm64-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
          IMAGE="harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog"

          # Docker login to Harbor
          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1 > /tmp/.hu
          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2 > /tmp/.hp
          docker login harbor.biswas.me -u "$(cat /tmp/.hu)" -p "$(cat /tmp/.hp)"
          rm -f /tmp/.hu /tmp/.hp

          # Native build (no cross-compilation)
          docker build --cache-from "$IMAGE:arm64-latest" -t "$IMAGE:$TAG" .
          docker push "$IMAGE:$TAG"

          # Tag as arm64-latest
          docker tag "$IMAGE:$TAG" "$IMAGE:arm64-latest"
          docker push "$IMAGE:arm64-latest"

          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Built and pushed $IMAGE:$TAG (ARM64)"

  # ── Build x64 Image (native on x64 runner) ───────────────────────────
  build-x64:
    name: "Build x64 Image"
    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64, prod]
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.build.outputs.tag }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build and push to Harbor
        id: build
        env:
          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
        run: |
          TAG="x64-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
          IMAGE="harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog"

          # Docker login to Harbor
          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1 > /tmp/.hu
          echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2 > /tmp/.hp
          docker login harbor.biswas.me -u "$(cat /tmp/.hu)" -p "$(cat /tmp/.hp)"
          rm -f /tmp/.hu /tmp/.hp

          # Native build (no cross-compilation)
          docker build --cache-from "$IMAGE:x64-latest" -t "$IMAGE:$TAG" .
          docker push "$IMAGE:$TAG"

          # Tag as x64-latest
          docker tag "$IMAGE:$TAG" "$IMAGE:x64-latest"
          docker push "$IMAGE:x64-latest"

          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Built and pushed $IMAGE:$TAG (x64)"

  # ── Deploy Staging (ARM64) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  deploy-staging:
    name: "Deploy to Staging"
    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, ARM64]
    needs: build-arm64
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: staging

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy to staging
        env:
          STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
          BLOG_PG_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.BLOG_PG_PASSWORD }}
          BLOG_SESSION_SECRET: ${{ secrets.BLOG_SESSION_SECRET }}
          BLOG_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOG_API_TOKEN }}
          STAGING_ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${{ secrets.STAGING_ENCRYPTION_KEY || secrets.ENCRYPTION_KEY }}
          GH_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_ID }}
          GH_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_SECRET }}
          OAUTH_STATE_SECRET: ${{ secrets.OAUTH_STATE_SECRET }}
          DD_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DD_API_KEY }}
          DD_SITE: ${{ secrets.DD_SITE }}
          VERSION_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VERSION_TOKEN }}
          IMAGE_TAG: ${{ needs.build-arm64.outputs.tag }}
        run: |
          # Use the ARM64 image tag built in the previous job
          echo "harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:${IMAGE_TAG}" > .image-tag.txt
          bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

  # ── Deploy UAT (ARM64, optional) ───────────────────────────────────────
  deploy-uat:
    name: "Deploy to UAT"
    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, ARM64]
    if: |
      (vars.CI_ENABLED || 'true') == 'true' &&
      github.ref == 'refs/heads/uat' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: uat

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy using arm64-latest image from Harbor
        env:
          UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64: ${{ secrets.UAT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
          BLOG_PG_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.BLOG_PG_PASSWORD }}
          BLOG_SESSION_SECRET: ${{ secrets.BLOG_SESSION_SECRET }}
          BLOG_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOG_API_TOKEN }}
          UAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${{ secrets.UAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY || secrets.ENCRYPTION_KEY }}
          GH_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_ID }}
          GH_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_SECRET }}
          OAUTH_STATE_SECRET: ${{ secrets.OAUTH_STATE_SECRET }}
          DD_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DD_API_KEY }}
          DD_SITE: ${{ secrets.DD_SITE }}
          VERSION_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VERSION_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          # Use arm64-latest from Harbor (already built on push to main)
          echo "harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:arm64-latest" > .image-tag.txt
          bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

  # ── Deploy Production (x64) ────────────────────────────────────────────
  deploy-production:
    name: "Deploy to Production"
    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64, prod]
    if: |
      (vars.CI_ENABLED || 'true') == 'true' &&
      github.ref == 'refs/heads/production' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: production

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy using x64-latest image from Harbor
        env:
          PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          HARBOR_AUTH: ${{ secrets.HARBOR_AUTH }}
          BLOG_PG_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.BLOG_PG_PASSWORD }}
          BLOG_SESSION_SECRET: ${{ secrets.BLOG_SESSION_SECRET }}
          BLOG_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOG_API_TOKEN }}
          PRODUCTION_ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_ENCRYPTION_KEY || secrets.ENCRYPTION_KEY }}
          GH_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_ID }}
          GH_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.GH_CLIENT_SECRET }}
          OAUTH_STATE_SECRET: ${{ secrets.OAUTH_STATE_SECRET }}
          DD_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DD_API_KEY }}
          DD_SITE: ${{ secrets.DD_SITE }}
          VERSION_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VERSION_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          # Use x64-latest from Harbor (already built on push to main)
          echo "harbor.biswas.me/biswas/blog:x64-latest" > .image-tag.txt
          bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me summarize and get alignment before executing.

## Current State

| Server | IP | Arch | Existing Runners |
|--------|-----|------|-----------------|
| Prod | 31.97.102.48 | **x86** | blog-production, aiagentlens-production |
| Staging | 129.213.82.37 | **ARM64** | pingrly-staging |
| UAT | 92.4.83.28 | **ARM64** | none |

**Important:** UAT is ARM64 (aarch64), not x86 as the plan assumed. So both staging and UAT are ARM64, only prod is x86.

## Projects to migrate (4 on Travis)

| Project | GitHub Repo | Needs Staging Runner | Needs Prod Runner |
|---------|------------|---------------------|-------------------|
| Blog | anchoo2kewl/blog | Yes | Already has one |
| TaskAI | anchoo2kewl/taskai | Yes | Yes |
| FlagTGL | anchoo2kewl/flagtgl | Yes | Yes |
| FolioWorth | anchoo2kewl/folioworth | Yes | Yes |

That's **7 new runner instances** to install (4 on staging, 3 on prod).

## Execution Plan

1. **Install runners** — Get registration tokens from GitHub, SSH to servers, install runner instances
2. **Create GHA workflows** — Use the blog's `ci-cd.yml` as template, adapt per project
3. **Migrate secrets** — Move Travis env vars to GitHub repo secrets
4. **Test** — Push to verify workflows run
5. **Remove `.travis.yml`** — Once confirmed working

Should I proceed? Any changes to the approach — e.g., do you want UAT runners too, or is SSH-based deploy from staging sufficient for UAT?

content.text:
User-level runners aren't supported by GitHub — only repo-level or **org-level** runners. To share 2 runners across 4+ repos, we need an organization.

Your current orgs: `calhounio`, `elastio`, `flagtgl`, `AIAgentLens`, `AI-AgentLens`

**Proposed approach:**
1. Create a new GitHub org (e.g., `biswas-dev` or similar) and transfer `blog`, `taskai`, `flagtgl`, `folioworth` there
2. Register **2 org-level runners on staging** (ARM64) and **2 on prod** (x86)
3. All repos in the org share those 4 runners

This changes repo URLs (e.g., `anchoo2kewl/taskai` → `<org>/taskai`) and GitHub auto-creates redirects for the old URLs.

Want me to proceed with this? What org name would you like?

input.command:
# Update local remotes for repos that are in ~/play
for dir in blog taskai flagtgl folioworth.com; do
  repo="${dir%.com}"  # folioworth.com -> folioworth
  if [ -d "$HOME/play/$dir/.git" ]; then
    old=$(git -C "$HOME/play/$dir" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
    git -C "$HOME/play/$dir" remote set-url origin "git@github.com:biswas-dev/$repo.git"
    new=$(git -C "$HOME/play/$dir" remote get-url origin)
    echo "$dir: $old → $new"
  fi
done

content.text: Good. Now I know the layout. Let me install org-level runners on all 3 servers. I need a fresh token since I'll run these in parallel.

input.command:
# Get a fresh org runner registration token
TOKEN=$(gh api -X POST /orgs/biswas-dev/actions/runners/registration-token --jq '.token')
echo "Token: $TOKEN"

# Runner version
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"

# Install on STAGING (ARM64) - runner name: staging-arm64
echo "=== Installing on staging (129.213.82.37, ARM64) ==="
ssh ubuntu@129.213.82.37 "
  mkdir -p ~/org-runner && cd ~/org-runner
  if [ ! -f ./config.sh ]; then
    curl -sL https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/actions-runner-linux-arm64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz | tar xz
  fi
  ./config.sh --url https://github.com/biswas-dev --token $TOKEN --name staging-arm64 --labels self-hosted,Linux,ARM64,staging --runnergroup Default --work _work --replace --unattended
  sudo ./svc.sh install
  sudo ./svc.sh start
  sudo ./svc.sh status
"

content.text: 3 down, 1 to go. Installing the second prod runner.

input.command:
# Install runner 2 on prod
TOKEN=$(gh api -X POST /orgs/biswas-dev/actions/runners/registration-token --jq '.token')
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"

ssh ubuntu@31.97.102.48 "
  mkdir -p ~/org-runner-2 && cd ~/org-runner-2
  if [ ! -f ./config.sh ]; then
    curl -sL https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz | tar xz
  fi
  ./config.sh --url https://github.com/biswas-dev --token $TOKEN --name prod-x64-2 --labels self-hosted,Linux,X64,prod --runnergroup Default --work _work --replace --unattended
  sudo ./svc.sh install
  sudo ./svc.sh start
  sudo ./svc.sh status
"
input.description: Install org runner 2 on prod server

file.content:
# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline
#
# FAST DEPLOY: Tests + deploy run in parallel. Deploy builds images directly
# on the target server (native arch, no cross-compilation, no registry).
# Tests run alongside deploy — if tests fail, the build is marked failed
# but staging already has the code (fix-forward).
#
# Pipeline:
#   main branch:       test + deploy-staging (parallel) → ~5 min
#   production branch: deploy-production → ~4 min (build on server)
#   uat branch:        deploy-uat → ~4 min (build on server)
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password") — kept for rollback
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: deploy-and-test
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: test-only
    if: type = pull_request
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # MAIN BRANCH: tests + staging deploy run IN PARALLEL (same stage)
    # Deploy builds natively on the staging server — no cross-compilation.
    # Tests run alongside — if they fail, build is marked failed but
    # staging is already deployed (fix-forward philosophy).
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-and-test
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: api-coverage
          paths:
            - api/coverage.out

    # ── Web Tests (Node) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-and-test
      name: "Web Tests"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: web-coverage
          paths:
            - web/coverage/

    # ── Deploy to Staging (builds on server, native ARM64) ────────────
    - stage: deploy-and-test
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy-fast.sh staging

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # PULL REQUESTS: tests only (no deploy)
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    - stage: test-only
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)

    - stage: test-only
      name: "Web Tests"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # UAT / PRODUCTION: build on server (native arch), no registry needed
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy-fast.sh uat

    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy-fast.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

file.content:
# Travis CI configuration for FlagTGL
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digest through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/flagtgl).
#
# Required env vars (set in Travis CI Settings):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   FLAGS_DB_PASSWORD, FLAGS_JWT_SECRET
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── Backend + SDK Tests (single Go job) ──────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Backend + SDK Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
      script:
        # Skip relay tests (pre-existing 403 failures, tracked separately)
        - cd backend && go vet ./... && go test -race -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /relay)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|flags/|backend/|g' coverage.out
        # SDK tests in same job (saves a whole VM)
        - cd ../sdks/go-server-sdk && go test ./... -count=1 -race
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: backend-coverage
          paths:
            - backend/coverage.out

    # ── Frontend + Docs + JS SDK (single Node job) ───────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Frontend + Docs + SDK (Node)"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      script:
        - cd frontend && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
        - cd ../docs && npm ci && npm run build
        - cd ../sdks/js-client-sdk && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit

    # ── SonarQube ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: sonarqube
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - backend-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ──────────────────────────────
    - stage: build-images
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: flagtgl-digest
          paths:
            - .image-digest.txt

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digest from build) ─────────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - flagtgl-digest
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ───────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production (Blue-Green)"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

content.content:
1→# Travis CI configuration for FolioWorth
     2→# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
     3→#
     4→# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
     5→# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/folioworth-{backend,frontend}).
     6→#
     7→# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
     8→#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
     9→#   FOLIOWORTH_PG_PASSWORD, FOLIOWORTH_JWT_SECRET, FOLIOWORTH_TINGO_API_KEY
    10→#   CF_ORIGIN_CERT_BASE64, CF_ORIGIN_KEY_BASE64
    11→#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
    12→#   SONAR_TOKEN
    13→
    14→language: minimal
    15→os: linux
    16→dist: jammy
    17→
    18→git:
    19→  depth: 10
    20→  submodules: false
    21→
    22→env:
    23→  global:
    24→    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc
    25→
    26→stages:
    27→  - name: test
    28→    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
    29→  - name: sonarqube
    30→    if: branch = main AND type = push

    32→    if: branch = main AND type = push

    40→jobs:

    42→    # ── Backend Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→    - stage: test

    45→      language: go
    46→      go: "1.24.2"
    47→      env:

    60→      workspaces:

    62→          name: backend-coverage

    65→
    66→    # ── Frontend Tests + Build (Node) ──────────────────────────────────────
    67→    - stage: test
    68→      name: "Frontend Tests + Build"
    69→      language: node_js
    70→      node_js: "24"
    71→      cache: npm
    72→      before_script:

    75→        - npx rollup --version || npm install @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu
    76→      script:
    77→        - npx tsc --noEmit
    78→        - npx vitest run --coverage
    79→        - npx vite build
    80→      workspaces:
    81→        create:
    82→          name: frontend-coverage
    83→          paths:
    84→            - frontend/coverage/
    85→
    86→    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    87→    - stage: sonarqube
    88→      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
    89→      git:
    90→        depth: false

    92→      workspaces:

    96→      script:

    98→          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
    99→          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
   100→          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
   101→          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
   102→          sonar-scanner \
   103→            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \

   105→            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"
   106→
   107→    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
   108→    - stage: build-images

file.content:
# Travis CI configuration for FolioWorth
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/folioworth-{backend,frontend}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   FOLIOWORTH_PG_PASSWORD, FOLIOWORTH_JWT_SECRET, FOLIOWORTH_TINGO_API_KEY
#   CF_ORIGIN_CERT_BASE64, CF_ORIGIN_KEY_BASE64
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── Backend Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Backend Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.24.2"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd backend
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: backend-coverage
          paths:
            - backend/coverage.out

    # ── Frontend Tests + Build (Node) ──────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Frontend Tests + Build"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd frontend
        - npm ci
        - npx rollup --version || npm install @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu
      script:
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
        - npx vite build
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: frontend-coverage
          paths:
            - frontend/coverage/

    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: sonarqube
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - backend-coverage
          - frontend-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
    - stage: build-images
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: folioworth-digests
          paths:
            - .image-digests.txt

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - folioworth-digests
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always
